text.title: Gemini Function Calling Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for Gemini function calling with manual-mode dispatch.

rule.statement: Every function used as a tool has a non-empty docstring describing intent + arguments. SDK uses the docstring as the tool description.
rule.rationale: Docstringless functions get auto-generated descriptions like "function foo" — model picks them poorly.

rule.statement: Every parameter has a Python type hint. SDK derives JSON Schema from annotations; missing hints get coerced to "string" or fail.
rule.rationale: Without type hints schema accuracy drops; model emits wrong types.

rule.statement: Production uses manual mode (caller dispatches functions). Automatic mode is acceptable for prototypes only.
rule.rationale: Auto mode bypasses validation, logging, and scope checks; production needs all three.

rule.statement: Validate LLM-supplied arguments before execution (jsonschema or pydantic against the function signature).
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate types; pre-execution validation prevents action on bad data.

rule.statement: Visible tool count ≤ 20 per call. Above that, route upstream.
For schema-constrained typed output (without forcing a tool), set response_mime_type="application/json" + response_schema. Do not rely on prompt-only JSON instructions.
rule.rationale: SDK-level mime+schema produces reliable shape; prompt-only drifts.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire Gemini function-calling loop.
Non-Gemini vendor or no tool need → skip.
